arts.ttl: Player seduces Chelsea, Blues ‘decide to take action’ to sign him – Club favour January exit
arts.sum: Chelsea have become the latest club to join the race to sign Paris Saint-Germain’s Randal Kolo Muani in the winter market, according to Foot Mercato. The forward has scored two goals and registered an assist from 14 games this season and four appearances in the past two months. arts.ttl: Opinion: Liam Delap set for big stage Chelsea audition tonight
arts.sum: Chelsea make the journey to Ipswich for our next Premier League game on Monday night this week as the busy festive period continues and we play our final match of 2024. After two poor performances in my opinion, we need to see a bit of a bounce back in this game, but it’s not going […]

arts.ttl: Lavia, Dewsbury-Hall, Fofana - Latest Chelsea injury news and return dates ahead of Ipswich Town - football.london
arts.sum: Maresca confirmed ahead of Fulham that the midfielder won't be fit for the game against Chelsea but he could feature against Ipswich. So with that in mind, football.london takes a look at the latest injury news from Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Chelsea can confirm double transfer in two days as £83m Nkunku swap deal emerges
arts.sum: football.london says: There is only a slight appeal to loaning Kolo Muani in January, and that is with the caveat that there is no obligation to buy. Having made the move from Eintracht Frankfurt, where Kolo Muani excelled and made a name for himself, catching the eye of Chelsea at the time, it has been hard going back in France.

arts.ttl: Chelsea XI vs Ipswich – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Chelsea make the trip to Ipswich Town this evening for the first time in over two decades. Enzo Maresca’s side are looking for a first win in three Premier League games, having stumbled of late with dropped points. arts.ttl: Ipswich Town vs Chelsea – Match preview and team news
arts.sum: Chelsea travel to Portman Road for the first time in 22 years to play Ipswich Town this evening. The hosts remained in the bottom three after they lost to Arsenal on Friday night. Massimo Luongo isn’t expected to feature for Kieran McKenna’s side due to illness. Axel Tuanzebe and Chiedozie Ogbene are long-term absentees. arts.ttl: I won the Champions League with Chelsea on my first start - now I tell them who to sign - football.london
arts.sum: In an interview with the Daily Mail, Bertrand, who won 19 caps for England across a career with the Blues, Southampton and Leicester City, and his business partner Louis Bell revealed how they already have Chelsea duo Levi Colwill and Armandro Broja on his books. Former Chelsea left-back Ryan Bertrand has revealed how he hopes to play a crucial role in developing Premier League talent through his sports agency, FA Sport.
